headlines.text: Trump Signs US-Iran Peace MOU at Versailles, Threatening to Bomb Again If Iran Misbehaves
Stocks Rebound as Iran Deal Lifts Sentiment; Fed Rate Hike Fear Caps Gains
Oil Tumbles Below $74 as Strait of Hormuz Set to Reopen
Gas at $3.99/Gallon Nationally — Still Up Over $1 Since War Began
JD Vance Holds White House Briefing, Insists US Has 'Great Leverage' Over Iran
Bill Pulte Named Acting US Spy Chief After Trump Calls Off Jay Clayton Hearing
Pentagon Names 8 Service Members Killed in B-52 Crash at Edwards AFB
World Cup 2026: England Beat Croatia, Messi Nets Hat Trick for Argentina
Nolan's 'The Odyssey' IMAX Tickets Crash AMC App — Scalpers List Seats for $1,000
Obama Presidential Center Opens in Chicago with Star-Studded Ceremony

breaking.text: Trump and Iran's Pezeshkian officially sign US-Iran Memorandum of Understanding at Versailles — 60-day countdown to final deal begins today

stories.hed: Trump Signs Iran MOU at Versailles, Vows Bombs Return If Tehran Misbehaves
stories.dek: Trump signed the 14-point US-Iran peace memo at the Palace of Versailles — the same room Woodrow Wilson signed the Treaty of Versailles, which historians say ended one war and started another. Critics on both sides are calling it a disaster; Trump is calling it genius. Sixty days of 'phase two' negotiations now begin, with nuclear enrichment, sanctions, and the Strait of Hormuz all still very much on the table.

stories.hed: Israel Keeps Striking Lebanon Despite Ceasefire Deal, Frustrating Trump and Vance
stories.dek: Even as the US-Iran MOU took effect, Israel struck Lebanon, killing one person and wounding three. Vance said Israel's strikes have repeatedly derailed US-Iran breakthrough moments, and Trump gets 'frustrated' when a ceasefire nears and 'a major explosion goes off in a civilian population center in Beirut.'

stories.hed: Bill Pulte Tapped as Acting US Spy Chief After Trump Scraps Jay Clayton Hearing
stories.dek: Trump's HUD director turned social media retribution machine will now have access to intelligence from all 18 US spy agencies. Republicans and Democrats alike opposed him, which in Washington apparently means he was the perfect pick.

stories.hed: Pentagon Names 8 Killed in B-52 Bomber Crash at Edwards AFB
stories.dek: The Pentagon released the names of eight US service members who died when a B-52 crashed shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in California on Monday. The Air Force is testing ways to keep its aging fleet of strategic bombers flying through at least 2050.

stories.hed: Trump Ended His Own War and Is Mad That Nobody Is Thanking Him Nicely Enough
stories.dek: At 4:42 a.m., fresh off his 3:30 a.m. landing at Joint Base Andrews, Trump posted that critics of his Iran deal are 'either jealous, bad people, or stupid' — a sentence that describes approximately every press conference he has ever held. Sen. Bill Cassidy called the war 'the worst foreign policy blunder in decades.' Trump called the stock market being up proof he was right. Both things can be true.

markets.context: Stocks bounced on the Iran peace deal while oil cratered and gold slipped as Fed rate-hike fears offset safe-haven demand — the market wanted peace, not another FOMC surprise.

gas.note: Pump prices are falling for the third straight week — great news unless you remember they were $2.96 before the war you just watched end on TV.

popCulture.hed: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' Crashes AMC App — IMAX Tickets Flip for $1,000 on Resale
popCulture.dek: Tickets for Nolan's $250M Homer adaptation starring Matt Damon, Zendaya, and Tom Holland went on sale this week, immediately overwhelming AMC, Fandango, and Regal with hour-long virtual queues. The film, shot entirely on IMAX cameras and opening July 17, is already being called the movie event of the decade — one X user asked why Odyssey tickets are harder to get than Coachella.

genZ.definition: The 'Saxophone Gets Louder' meme is June 2026's dominant TikTok format: a creator sets up a normal situation, then the moment everything goes catastrophically wrong, a blaring sax solo erupts at full volume while the video zooms in or freezes. It's the digital equivalent of a dramatic 80s movie breakdown and has taken over every corner of the FYP this month.
genZ.usage: Trump landing at Joint Base Andrews at 3:30 a.m. to immediately post 'jealous, bad people, or stupid' — *saxophone gets louder*.

tldr: Trump signed a 14-point Iran peace memo at Versailles at midnight and was back on US soil posting unhinged tweets by 4:42 a.m. — markets celebrated with stocks up and oil crashing, while the rest of us quietly wondered if a deal negotiated between a G7 dinner and a red-eye flight will hold for 60 days.
